growth_records.medium: Defined bicarbonate-buffered anaerobic mineral medium (DSMZ-based) with 25 mM ethanol

up.method: empirical maximum consumption rate (qS,max) from batch fermentation profile (36.2 +/- 5.5)
co-consumed with ethanol during exponential phase (stated 'not shown', not quantified)

sec.method: empirical maximum production rate (qP,max) 12.0 +/- 0.1; final titre 9.5 mM
empirical maximum production rate (qA,max) 8.6 +/- 0.5; final titre 8.6 mM
minor product detected at 1.3 mM final concentration (rate not reported)
minor product detected at 1 mM final concentration (rate not reported)

growth_records.cite: Benito-Vaquerizo, Sara et al. Genome-scale metabolic modelling enables deciphering ethanol metabolism via the acrylate pathway in the propionate-producer Acetobacterium neopropionicum. Microbial Cell Factories (2022). DOI:10.1186/s12934-022-01841-1. [PMC9205015]
growth_records.snippet: During the exponential phase, ethanol was uptaken ... at qS,max of 36.2 +/- 5.5 mmol ethanol gDW-1 h-1 ... qP,max and qA,max of 12.0 +/- 0.1 mmol propionate gDW-1 h-1 and 8.6 +/- 0.5 mmol acetate gDW-1 h-1 ... mu_max was 0.082 +/- 0.006 h-1
growth_records.notes: Ethanol-only (25 mM) + CO2 batch condition. All rates experimentally measured. mu_max reported 0.082+/-0.006 h-1, td reported 8.4 h (consistent: ln2/0.082=8.45 h). YX/S 1.6 gDW/mol; biomass peaked 44.7+/-1.3 mgDW/L at ~47 h. dFBA-modelled values excluded; only empirical q's captured.

growth_records.medium: Defined bicarbonate-buffered anaerobic mineral medium with 25 mM ethanol + 10 mM acetate

sec.method: empirical qP,max 16.4 +/- 0.8 (37% higher vs no acetate); final titre 11.3 mM
empirical qA,max 3.1 +/- 0.6 net production despite 10 mM acetate added at start; final titre 16.7 mM

growth_records.cite: Benito-Vaquerizo, Sara et al. Genome-scale metabolic modelling enables deciphering ethanol metabolism via the acrylate pathway in the propionate-producer Acetobacterium neopropionicum. Microbial Cell Factories (2022). DOI:10.1186/s12934-022-01841-1. [PMC9205015]
growth_records.snippet: mu was 0.098 +/- 0.005 h-1 (duplication time = 7.1 h); ... qS,max was 43.3 +/- 4.3 mmol ethanol gDW h-1 ... qA,max ... 3.1 +/- 0.6 ... qP,max ... 16.4 +/- 0.8 mmol propionate gDW h-1
growth_records.notes: Ethanol + 10 mM acetate condition. Acetate added as co-substrate but net produced (qA,max positive). All q's experimentally measured. mu_max 0.098+/-0.005 (td 7.1 h; ln2/0.098=7.07 h consistent). Max biomass 41.1+/-0.8 mgDW/L (~9% lower than acetate-free).

growth_records.medium: Defined bicarbonate-buffered anaerobic mineral medium with 25 mM ethanol + 25 mM acetate

up.method: qS,max stated 'similar to the condition without acetate' (~36 mmol/gDW/h); exact value data not shown

sec.method: qP,max stated similar to no-acetate condition (~12 mmol/gDW/h, data not shown); highest final titre of the three conditions at 12.5 mM
qA,max stated similar to no-acetate condition (data not shown)

growth_records.cite: Benito-Vaquerizo, Sara et al. Genome-scale metabolic modelling enables deciphering ethanol metabolism via the acrylate pathway in the propionate-producer Acetobacterium neopropionicum. Microbial Cell Factories (2022). DOI:10.1186/s12934-022-01841-1. [PMC9205015]
growth_records.snippet: The incubations containing 25 mM acetate at the start ... the mu_max, qP,max and qA,max were similar to the condition without acetate (data not shown). The final propionate concentration was 12.5 mM, the highest of the three conditions test
growth_records.notes: Ethanol + 25 mM acetate condition. mu_max/qP,max/qA,max explicitly stated similar to acetate-free condition (mu~0.082 h-1) but exact numbers 'data not shown' -> growth_rate/td carried from no-acetate value as approximation, rates left null. Only yields reported numerically: YX/S 1.4 gDW/mol, YP/S 0.42, YA/S 0.06 mol/mol. Highest propionate yield/titre of the series.
